findings.problem: The opening kitchen scene already lands the chapter's new register by line 63, but the chapter then spends most of its remaining length certifying the same tenderness in slightly different forms: shared meals, library domesticity, cataloguing Hermione, ordinary lunch, then the closing inventory of kettle/laugh/household warmth. Because those sections mostly restate one state change instead of staging distinct new tests, costs, or irreversible choices, the chapter's center of gravity becomes recurrence and sprawl rather than accumulation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the montage beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:67 and the cataloguing sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:117 so each retained block does non-overlapping work. If you keep the library sleep beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:171, make it the chapter's first real escalation in Draco's behavior; if you keep Theo's visit at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:279, let that scene supply the external witness/public consequence instead of using the closing summary at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:345 as another pass of the same proof.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can point to one distinct dramatic function in each surviving major block after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:67: one block establishes the new routine, one block escalates Draco into a behavior he would not have allowed earlier, and Theo's block changes the situation by giving that shift an outside witness. Fail if the revised middle still reads as multiple paraphrases of 'they are gentler now' without a new action, cost, or decision.

findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly explains the meaning of a beat immediately after dramatizing it. The loaded look at the kettle, the first offered repair, the new meal routine, Draco watching Hermione work, the ordinary lunch, and the final cleanup all arrive on the page and then receive an interpretive gloss telling the reader what changed. That after-the-fact translation weakens the pressure of scenes that were already legible.
findings.rewrite_direction: At the beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:345, cut or roughen the explanatory follow-through so the scene can land on action, image, or dialogue. When narration remains, make it advance Draco's self-knowledge beyond what the reader already inferred rather than restating the same emotional conclusion in cleaner language.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each cited beat lands on an observable image, action, or line of dialogue that carries the turn without an immediate abstract paraphrase of the same meaning in the next sentence. Fail if the revision still tells the reader what the silence, lunch, or warmth 'really' means after the page has already shown it.

findings.problem: A marked prose scaffold recurs mechanically across the chapter: 'the specific quality,' 'the particular state,' 'the specific walk,' 'the specific ease,' 'the specific quality,' 'the particular warmth,' 'Theo's specific quiet.' The repeated specific/particular intensifier becomes a default emphasis marker rather than a deliberate choice, which flattens otherwise strong observation into recognizable autopilot.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the sentences anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:13, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:137, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:219, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:325 so the emphasis comes from the noun, verb, or image itself rather than from the same qualifying scaffold. Strategy options: delete the qualifier where the concrete detail already carries weight, or swap in fresh sentence architecture that does not lean on 'specific/particular' as a precision crutch.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the cited beats, no more than one 'specific/particular [noun]' intensifier remains and the remaining observations are differentiated by fresh diction and syntax rather than the same qualifier pattern. Fail if the revised chapter still uses that scaffold as its default way of sounding precise.

findings.problem: The chapter's dialogue is mostly character-true, but several showcase lines slip into composed register. The audit turns up thesis-speech and aphoristic/quotable compression in Draco's 'We're standing in the proof' beat, a polished diagnostic binary in the Hermione/Theo licensing exchange, and a mini-speech diagnosis built around Theo's mic-drop line 'You look like someone who lives here.' There is not a literal rhetorical-question mic drop or narrator admiration pattern driving the chapter, but these lines still land as written-about thematic seals rather than speech arriving under social pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the exchanges at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:87, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:289, and especially the goodbye sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:327. Theo can still see the truth, but he should reach it sideways, with interruption, hedging, self-correction, or redirected humor; likewise, Draco and Hermione should not get a perfectly balanced thesis line when a messier, more interruptible version would suit the scene pressure better.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:327 contains audible friction such as interruption, aborted phrasing, or deflective humor, and if at least one of the polished lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:87 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:289 is rewritten so it no longer reads as a stand-alone maxim or tidy diagnosis. Fail if Theo still exits on an equivalently neat thesis sentence or if the lunch exchange still resolves into polished debate copy.

findings.problem: A calibration-only style-bible example line appears verbatim in the chapter: 'Sure.' The reuse happens twice, once in Hermione's dialogue and once in Theo's. Even though the copied material is only a one-word reply, the audit rule is explicit that example lines are non-copyable and should not survive unchanged in manuscript text.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the replies at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:59 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:217 with character-true alternatives, then do a final pass against outline/style_bible.json to ensure no `example_lines` entry appears verbatim anywhere in chapter 10.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if no line of dialogue in chapter 10 is verbatim identical to any `example_lines` entry in outline/style_bible.json. Fail if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:59 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:217 still reads 'Sure.' or if any other calibration-only example survives unchanged.

summary: Chapter 10 keeps continuity, world rules, and past-tense control intact, and the core Draco/Hermione/Theo voices are recognizable. It still fails pass-level review because the chapter lands its state shift early and then spends too much of its length re-certifying the same tenderness in polished explanatory language; the result is sprawl, overstatement, a recurrent prose tic, and a few dialogue beats that feel composed rather than overheard. One style-bible calibration line is also copied verbatim into the manuscript.
